Name: k_cell_clear
Definition: Clearance rate of dead/apoptotic cells from tumor compartment, used in multiple cell death pathways (cancer, T cells, macrophages, fibroblasts, MDSCs)

Name: cell
Definition: SimBiology unit parameter representing individual cells, mapped to 'molecule' unit internally

Name: day
Definition: SimBiology unit parameter for time scale, mapped to 'second' with conversion factor internally

Name: vol_cell
Definition: Average cancer cell volume calculated from diameter, used in tumor volume equation V_T and initial conditions

Name: vol_Tcell
Definition: Average T cell volume calculated from diameter, contributes to total tumor volume alongside cancer cells

Name: Ve_T
Definition: Cellular volume fraction of tumor tissue, used as denominator in tumor volume calculation V_T = (cells*vol_cell)/Ve_T

Name: C_total
Definition: Total cancer cell count across all phenotypes (C1+C2), calculated variable used in growth dynamics and T cell interactions

Name: T_total
Definition: Total activated CD8+ T cell count in tumor compartment, calculated variable used in cytotoxicity and recruitment dynamics

Name: T_total_LN
Definition: Total activated T cell count in tumor-draining lymph nodes, calculated variable for tracking immune activation

Name: R_Tcell
Definition: Calculated rate of cancer cell cytotoxicity by CD8+ T cells, derived from k_C_CD8 baseline rate modified by checkpoint inhibition (PD-1/PD-L1, CTLA-4) and ECM-mediated suppression Hill functions

Name: Tregs_
Definition: Total regulatory T cell count, calculated variable used in T effector suppression (K_T_Treg parameter)

Name: H_APC
Definition: Hill function for APC-mediated Treg activation by self-antigens (P0), calculated from mature APC levels and P0 presentation in lymph nodes using TCR-MHC binding dynamics

Name: H_mAPC
Definition: Hill function for mature APC-mediated CD8+ T cell activation by tumor antigens (P1), calculated from mature APC levels and P1 presentation in lymph nodes using TCR-MHC binding dynamics

Name: H_APCh
Definition: Hill function for mature APC-mediated CD4+ helper T cell activation by tumor neoantigens, calculated from mature APC levels and neoantigen presentation in lymph nodes

Name: H_PD1_C1
Definition: Hill function for PD-1/PD-L1 checkpoint inhibition of T cells by chemotherapy-sensitive cancer cells, calculated from receptor-ligand binding dynamics

Name: H_PD1_APC
Definition: Hill function for PD-1/PD-L1 checkpoint inhibition of T cells by APCs, calculated from receptor-ligand binding dynamics

Name: H_CD28_C1
Definition: Hill function for CD28 costimulatory signaling from chemotherapy-sensitive cancer cells, calculated from CD28-CD80/CD86 binding

Name: H_CD28_APC
Definition: Hill function for CD28 costimulatory signaling from APCs, calculated from CD28-CD80/CD86 binding dynamics

Name: k_C1_growth
Definition: Intrinsic growth rate of chemotherapy-sensitive cancer clone, used in Gompertzian/logistic growth models

Name: k_C1_death
Definition: Baseline apoptotic death rate of chemotherapy-sensitive cancer clone, independent of immune or therapy effects

Name: C_max
Definition: Tumor carrying capacity for logistic growth, dynamically updated to vasculature capacity V_T.K in model

Name: k_C1_therapy
Definition: Therapy-induced death rate for chemotherapy-sensitive cancer cells, represents additional mortality from cytotoxic drugs beyond baseline apoptosis

Name: initial_tumour_diameter
Definition: Initial tumor diameter used to calculate starting cancer cell number from volume, varied in PSA (1.43-3.03 cm)

Name: k_K_g
Definition: Rate of tumor vasculature growth driven by angiogenic factors, used in vascular capacity expansion model K dynamics

Name: k_K_d
Definition: Rate of tumor vasculature degradation/collapse proportional to tumor burden, used in vascular capacity reduction model

Name: k_vas_Csec
Definition: Rate of angiogenic factor secretion by cancer cells (VEGF-like factors), drives tumor vasculature growth through k_K_g pathway

Name: k_vas_deg
Definition: Degradation rate of angiogenic factors in tumor microenvironment, regulates vascular growth signaling duration

Name: c_vas_50
Definition: Half-maximal angiogenic factor concentration for tumor vasculature growth, determines sensitivity of vascular expansion to angiogenic stimuli

Name: k_C2_growth
Definition: Intrinsic growth rate of chemotherapy-resistant cancer clone

Name: k_C2_death
Definition: Baseline apoptotic death rate of chemotherapy-resistant cancer clone

Name: k_C2_therapy
Definition: Therapy-induced death rate for chemotherapy-resistant cancer cells, typically much lower than sensitive clone due to resistance mechanisms

Name: div_Tregs
Definition: T cell receptor diversity for regulatory T cells, representing repertoire size for self-antigen recognition

Name: n_Treg_clones
Definition: Number of regulatory T cell clones recognizing self-antigens (P0), determines the fraction of Treg repertoire that can be activated by tissue-specific antigens in lymph nodes

Name: q_nTreg_LN_in
Definition: Rate of naive regulatory T cell transport into tumor-draining lymph nodes, determines the naive Treg influx available for activation by self-antigens presented by APCs

Name: q_Treg_LN_out
Definition: Rate of activated regulatory T cell egress from lymph nodes to peripheral circulation, allows Tregs to migrate to tumor sites after activation by self-antigens

Name: q_nTreg_LN_out
Definition: Rate of naive regulatory T cell egress from lymph nodes without activation, represents baseline Treg recirculation through lymphoid organs

Name: k_Treg_act
Definition: Rate of naive Treg activation by APCs presenting self-antigens (P0), depends on TCR-MHC class II binding strength and costimulatory signals
References: De Boer and Perelson 1995, PMID: 7475092; Robertson-Tessi 2012, PMID: 22051568

Name: k_Treg_pro
Definition: Activated regulatory T cell proliferation rate in lymph nodes, enhanced by IL-2 secreted by activated T effector cells (IL2_50_Treg parameter)

Name: k_Treg_death
Definition: Regulatory T cell apoptotic death rate in all compartments, represents natural turnover independent of activation state

Name: q_Treg_P_in
Definition: Rate of activated Treg transport from central to peripheral compartments via vascular transmigration, allows systemic Treg distribution

Name: q_Treg_P_out
Definition: Rate of Treg egress from peripheral compartment back to central circulation, maintains Treg recirculation between compartments

Name: q_Treg_T_in
Definition: Rate of activated regulatory T cell infiltration into tumor tissue, depends on tumor vasculature density and adhesion molecule expression

Name: q_nTreg_P_in
Definition: Rate of naive regulatory T cell transport from central to peripheral compartments, maintains naive Treg distribution throughout body

Name: q_nTreg_P_out
Definition: Rate of naive regulatory T cell egress from peripheral compartment back to central circulation, maintains steady-state naive Treg distribution

Name: Q_nTreg_thym
Definition: Thymic output rate of naive regulatory T cells, represents de novo Treg production that maintains peripheral Treg pool size throughout life

Name: k_nTreg_pro
Definition: Maximum rate of naive regulatory T cell homeostatic proliferation in peripheral compartments, maintains Treg pool size when thymic output declines

Name: K_nTreg_pro
Definition: Half-maximal naive Treg number for homeostatic proliferation saturation, determines density-dependent regulation of naive Treg expansion

Name: k_nTreg_death
Definition: Naive regulatory T cell natural death rate, represents baseline apoptosis in absence of activation signals
References: Estimated from T cell turnover studies

Name: k_IL2_deg
Definition: Rate of IL-2 cytokine degradation in lymph node compartment, regulates IL-2 availability for T cell proliferation enhancement and Treg activation

Name: k_IL2_cons
Definition: Rate of IL-2 consumption by activated T cells, represents uptake through high-affinity IL-2 receptors (CD25/CD122/CD132) for proliferation signaling

Name: k_IL2_sec
Definition: Rate of IL-2 secretion by activated CD8+ and CD4+ T cells in lymph nodes, provides autocrine and paracrine proliferation signals

Name: IL2_50
Definition: Half-maximal IL-2 concentration for CD8+ T cell proliferation enhancement, determines additional division rounds (N_IL2_CD8) beyond baseline TCR-driven proliferation (N0)

Name: IL2_50_Treg
Definition: Half-maximal IL-2 concentration for regulatory T cell proliferation and activation, determines Treg expansion in response to IL-2 signaling from activated T effector cells

Name: N0
Definition: Baseline number of T cell divisions from TCR signaling alone (without costimulation or IL-2)

Name: N_costim
Definition: Additional T cell divisions enabled by costimulatory signals (CD28-CD80/86)

Name: N_IL2_CD8
Definition: Maximum additional CD8+ T cell divisions enabled by IL-2 signaling beyond baseline

Name: N_IL2_CD4
Definition: Maximum additional CD4+ T cell divisions enabled by IL-2 signaling beyond baseline

Name: k_Treg
Definition: Rate of CD8+ T effector cell suppression/death mediated by regulatory T cells, represents Treg-mediated immunosuppression through contact-dependent and cytokine mechanisms

Name: H_P0
Definition: Hill function for self-antigen P0 presentation by APCs to regulatory T cells, calculated from TCR-MHC class II-peptide complex formation and determines Treg activation strength

Name: N_aCD8
Definition: Number of activated CD8+ T cell proliferation generations, calculated as N0 + N_costim*H_CD28 + N_IL2_CD8*IL2_effect, determines total progeny from single activation event

Name: N_aTreg
Definition: Number of activated regulatory T cell proliferation generations, calculated as N0 + N_costim*H_CD28 + N_IL2_CD4*IL2_effect, determines Treg expansion capacity

Name: div_CD8
Definition: CD8+ T cell receptor diversity representing total repertoire size for tumor antigen recognition, used to normalize clone numbers and activation probabilities

Name: n_CD8_clones
Definition: Number of CD8+ T cell clones recognizing tumor-specific antigens (P1), determines the fraction of CD8+ repertoire available for anti-tumor responses

Name: q_nCD8_LN_in
Definition: Rate of naive CD8+ T cell entry into tumor-draining lymph nodes, determines the naive precursor influx available for tumor antigen presentation and activation

Name: q_CD8_LN_out
Definition: Rate of activated CD8+ T cell egress from lymph nodes to systemic circulation, allows activated T cells to reach tumor sites after antigen recognition

Name: q_nCD8_LN_out
Definition: Rate of naive CD8+ T cell egress from lymph nodes without activation, represents baseline T cell recirculation through lymphoid organs

Name: k_CD8_act
Definition: Rate of naive CD8+ T cell activation by mature APCs presenting tumor antigens (P1), depends on TCR-MHC class I binding strength and costimulatory signal availability
References: De Boer and Perelson 1995, PMID: 7475092; Robertson-Tessi 2012, PMID: 22051568

Name: k_CD8_pro
Definition: Activated CD8+ T cell proliferation rate in lymph nodes, enhanced by IL-2 availability and costimulatory signals, generates effector T cell progeny

Name: k_CD8_death
Definition: CD8+ T cell apoptotic death rate in all compartments, represents natural turnover independent of activation or exhaustion mechanisms

Name: q_CD8_P_in
Definition: Rate of activated CD8+ T cell transport from central to peripheral compartments via vascular transmigration, enables systemic T cell distribution

Name: q_CD8_P_out
Definition: Rate of CD8+ T cell egress from peripheral compartment back to central circulation, maintains T cell recirculation between compartments

Name: q_CD8_T_in
Definition: Rate of activated CD8+ T cell infiltration into tumor tissue, depends on tumor vasculature, chemokine gradients, and cancer cell density for recruitment

Name: q_nCD8_P_in
Definition: Rate of naive CD8+ T cell transport from central to peripheral compartments, maintains naive T cell distribution throughout body tissues

Name: q_nCD8_P_out
Definition: Rate of naive CD8+ T cell egress from peripheral compartment back to central circulation, maintains steady-state naive T cell distribution across body tissues

Name: Q_nCD8_thym
Definition: Thymic output rate of naive CD8+ T cells, represents de novo T cell production that maintains peripheral CD8+ T cell pool size throughout life

Name: k_nCD8_pro
Definition: Maximum rate of naive CD8+ T cell homeostatic proliferation in peripheral compartments, maintains T cell pool size when thymic output declines with age

Name: K_nCD8_pro
Definition: Half-maximal naive CD8+ T cell number for homeostatic proliferation saturation, determines density-dependent regulation of naive T cell expansion

Name: k_nCD8_death
Definition: Naive CD8+ T cell natural death rate, represents baseline apoptosis in absence of activation signals or pathological conditions
References: Estimated from T cell turnover studies

Name: k_CD8_exh
Definition: Rate of CD8+ T cell exhaustion in tumor microenvironment, represents transition to functionally impaired state due to chronic antigen exposure and inhibitory signals

Name: k_C_CD8
Definition: Baseline rate of cancer cell cytotoxicity by CD8+ T cells through perforin/granzyme and FasL pathways, modified by checkpoint inhibition (H_PD1) and ECM barriers (H_ECM_T_mot)

Name: k_Tcell_ECM
Definition: Rate of T cell exhaustion induced by extracellular matrix components (collagen, fibronectin), represents mechanical and biochemical barriers to T cell function in fibrotic tumors
References: Fitted to Ma 2022 single cell data

Name: H_ECM_T_mot
Definition: Hill function for ECM-mediated reduction in CD8+ T cell motility and cancer killing efficiency, calculated as ECM_level/(ECM_50_T_mot+ECM_level), represents physical barrier effects

Name: H_ECM_T_exh
Definition: Hill function for ECM-mediated CD8+ T cell exhaustion, calculated as ECM_level/(ECM_50_T_exh+ECM_level), represents biochemical exhaustion signals from fibrotic environment

Name: k_IFNg_Tsec
Definition: Rate of IFN-γ secretion by activated CD8+ T cells in tumor, enhances PD-L1 expression (r_PDL1_IFNg) and macrophage M1 polarization

Name: H_P1
Definition: Hill function for tumor-specific antigen P1 presentation by APCs to CD8+ T cells, calculated from TCR-MHC class I-peptide complex formation, determines CD8+ T cell activation strength

Name: K_T_C
Definition: Michaelis-Menten constant for T cell cytotoxicity saturation with respect to effector-to-target ratio, determines efficiency of CD8+ T cell killing at different T cell densities

Name: K_T_Treg
Definition: Michaelis-Menten constant for Treg-mediated suppression of CD8+ T effector function, determines sensitivity of T effector suppression to Treg density in tumor microenvironment

Name: k_APC_mat
Definition: Maximum rate of APC maturation from immature to mature antigen-presenting state, triggered by danger signals, cytokines, or pathogen-associated molecular patterns

Name: k_APC_mig
Definition: Rate of mature APC migration from tumor to tumor-draining lymph nodes, enables tumor antigen presentation to naive T cells for priming

Name: k_APC_death
Definition: Rate of immature APC death in tumor compartment, represents natural turnover of tissue-resident dendritic cells

Name: k_mAPC_death
Definition: Rate of mature APC death in lymph nodes after antigen presentation, higher than immature APCs due to activation-induced cell death

Name: APC0_T
Definition: Steady-state density of CD103+ dendritic cells in tumor tissue, represents baseline antigen-presenting capacity for tumor antigen uptake and cross-presentation

Name: APC0_LN
Definition: Steady-state density of CD103+ dendritic cells in tumor-draining lymph nodes, provides baseline antigen presentation capacity for T cell priming

Name: n_sites_APC
Definition: Maximum number of T cells that can simultaneously form immunological synapses with a single APC, determines APC antigen presentation capacity

Name: kin
Definition: Rate of MHC class I/II internalization from APC surface for peptide loading, part of antigen processing and presentation pathway

Name: kout
Definition: Rate of peptide-loaded MHC class I/II externalization to APC surface for T cell recognition, completes antigen presentation cycle

Name: k_P0_up
Definition: Rate of self-antigen P0 uptake by APCs from dying cells, initiates processing and presentation pathway for regulatory T cell activation

Name: k_xP0_deg
Definition: Rate of extracellular self-antigen P0 degradation in tissue, competes with APC uptake and determines antigen availability for presentation

Name: k_P0_deg
Definition: Rate of self-antigen P0 degradation within APC endosomes, competes with MHC class II loading and determines efficiency of Treg activation

Name: k_p0_deg
Definition: Rate of processed self-antigen P0 epitope degradation in endosomes, determines half-life of peptides available for MHC class II loading

Name: k_P0_on
Definition: Rate of self-antigen P0 epitope binding to MHC class II molecules in APC endosomes, determines presentation efficiency to regulatory T cells

Name: k_P0_d1
Definition: Dissociation constant for self-antigen P0-MHC class II binding, determines stability and presentation strength of P0 epitopes to Tregs

Name: p0_50
Definition: Half-maximal self-antigen P0-MHC class II density for regulatory T cell activation in immunological synapse, determines Treg activation threshold

Name: P0_C1
Definition: Intracellular concentration of self-antigen P0 in chemotherapy-sensitive cancer cells, released upon cell death to activate regulatory T cells

Name: P0_C2
Definition: Intracellular concentration of self-antigen P0 in chemotherapy-resistant cancer cells, released upon cell death to activate regulatory T cells

Name: A_syn
Definition: Surface area of immunological synapse between T cell and APC/cancer cell, determines contact area for receptor-ligand interactions and signaling

Name: A_Tcell
Definition: Total surface area of T cell membrane, used to calculate receptor densities and normalize checkpoint molecule expression levels

Name: A_cell
Definition: Total surface area of cancer cell membrane, used to calculate checkpoint molecule densities (PD-L1, CD80, CD86) and normalize expression levels

Name: A_APC
Definition: Total surface area of antigen-presenting cell membrane, used to calculate MHC and costimulatory molecule densities for T cell activation

Name: k_M1p0_TCR_on
Definition: Rate of TCR binding to self-antigen P0-MHC class II complex on APC surface, initiates regulatory T cell signaling cascade

Name: k_M1p0_TCR_off
Definition: Rate of TCR dissociation from P0-MHC class II complex, determines duration of Treg signaling and activation strength

Name: k_M1p0_TCR_p
Definition: Rate of conformational changes in P0-MHC-TCR complex during signaling, modulates signal strength and Treg activation efficiency

Name: phi_M1p0_TCR
Definition: Rate of P0-MHC-TCR complex transition to non-signaling state after maximal modification, terminates Treg activation signal

Name: N_M1p0_TCR
Definition: Number of sequential conformational modification steps in P0-MHC-TCR signaling complex, determines signal amplification and duration

Name: TCR_p0_tot
Definition: Total TCR density on naive regulatory T cell surface available for self-antigen P0 recognition, determines maximum binding capacity

Name: pTCR_p0_MHC_tot
Definition: Total density of P0-MHC-TCR complexes at all modification states in immunological synapse, calculated variable representing Treg signaling strength

Name: k_P1_up
Definition: Rate of tumor-specific antigen P1 uptake by APCs from dying cancer cells, initiates cross-presentation pathway for CD8+ T cell activation

Name: k_xP1_deg
Definition: Rate of extracellular tumor antigen P1 degradation in tissue, competes with APC uptake and determines antigen availability for cross-presentation

Name: k_P1_deg
Definition: Rate of tumor antigen P1 degradation within APC endosomes, competes with MHC class I loading and determines CD8+ T cell priming efficiency

Name: k_p1_deg
Definition: Rate of processed tumor antigen P1 epitope degradation in endosomes, determines half-life of peptides available for MHC class I loading

Name: k_P1_on
Definition: Rate of tumor antigen P1 epitope binding to MHC class I molecules in APC endosomes, determines cross-presentation efficiency to CD8+ T cells

Name: k_P1_d1
Definition: Dissociation constant for tumor antigen P1-MHC class I binding, determines stability and presentation strength of P1 epitopes to CD8+ T cells

Name: p1_50
Definition: Half-maximal tumor antigen P1-MHC class I density for CD8+ T cell activation in immunological synapse, determines T effector activation threshold

Name: P1_C1
Definition: Intracellular concentration of tumor-specific antigen P1 in chemotherapy-sensitive cancer cells, released upon cell death for cross-presentation to CD8+ T cells

Name: P1_C2
Definition: Intracellular concentration of tumor-specific antigen P1 in chemotherapy-resistant cancer cells, released upon cell death for cross-presentation to CD8+ T cells

Name: k_M1p1_TCR_on
Definition: Rate of CD8+ TCR binding to tumor antigen P1-MHC class I complex on APC surface, initiates CD8+ T cell activation cascade

Name: k_M1p1_TCR_off
Definition: Rate of CD8+ TCR dissociation from P1-MHC class I complex, determines duration of activation signaling and T effector priming strength

Name: k_M1p1_TCR_p
Definition: Rate of conformational changes in P1-MHC-TCR complex during CD8+ T cell signaling, modulates activation signal strength and effector differentiation

Name: phi_M1p1_TCR
Definition: Rate of P1-MHC-TCR complex transition to non-signaling state after maximal modification, terminates CD8+ T cell activation signal

Name: N_M1p1_TCR
Definition: Number of sequential conformational modification steps in P1-MHC-TCR signaling complex, determines CD8+ T cell activation signal amplification

Name: TCR_p1_tot
Definition: Total TCR density on naive CD8+ T cell surface available for tumor antigen P1 recognition, determines maximum binding capacity for activation

Name: pTCR_p1_MHC_tot
Definition: Total density of P1-MHC-TCR complexes at all modification states in immunological synapse, calculated variable representing CD8+ T cell signaling strength

Name: q_P_aPD1
Definition: Capillary filtration rate of anti-PD-1 antibody from central to peripheral compartment, determines systemic drug distribution for checkpoint blockade

Name: q_T_aPD1
Definition: Capillary filtration rate of anti-PD-1 antibody from central to tumor compartment, determines tumor penetration for T cell checkpoint inhibition

Name: q_LN_aPD1
Definition: Capillary filtration rate of anti-PD-1 antibody from central to tumor-draining lymph node compartment, enables T cell priming enhancement

Name: q_LD_aPD1
Definition: Rate of anti-PD-1 antibody lymphatic drainage from tumor-draining lymph nodes back to central circulation, completes drug distribution cycle

Name: k_cl_aPD1
Definition: Systemic clearance rate of anti-PD-1 antibody from central compartment, determines drug half-life and dosing requirements for sustained checkpoint blockade

Name: gamma_C_aPD1
Definition: Volume fraction of central compartment interstitial space accessible to anti-PD-1 antibody, determines drug distribution volume and concentration

Name: gamma_P_aPD1
Definition: Volume fraction of peripheral compartment interstitial space accessible to anti-PD-1 antibody, affects tissue drug penetration and efficacy

Name: gamma_T_aPD1
Definition: Volume fraction of tumor interstitial space accessible to anti-PD-1 antibody, determines intratumoral drug penetration for T cell checkpoint inhibition

Name: gamma_LN_aPD1
Definition: Volume fraction of lymph node interstitial space accessible to anti-PD-1 antibody, affects drug availability for T cell priming enhancement

Name: q_P_aPDL1
Definition: Capillary filtration rate of anti-PD-L1 antibody from central to peripheral compartment, determines systemic drug distribution for checkpoint blockade

Name: q_T_aPDL1
Definition: Capillary filtration rate of anti-PD-L1 antibody from central to tumor compartment, determines tumor penetration for cancer cell checkpoint inhibition

Name: q_LN_aPDL1
Definition: Capillary filtration rate of anti-PD-L1 antibody from central to tumor-draining lymph node compartment, enables APC checkpoint inhibition

Name: q_LD_aPDL1
Definition: Rate of anti-PD-L1 antibody lymphatic drainage from tumor-draining lymph nodes back to central circulation, completes drug distribution cycle

Name: k_cl_aPDL1
Definition: Systemic clearance rate of anti-PD-L1 antibody from central compartment, determines drug half-life and dosing requirements for sustained checkpoint blockade

Name: gamma_C_aPDL1
Definition: Volume fraction of central compartment interstitial space accessible to anti-PD-L1 antibody, determines drug distribution volume and concentration

Name: gamma_P_aPDL1
Definition: Volume fraction of peripheral compartment interstitial space accessible to anti-PD-L1 antibody, affects tissue drug penetration and efficacy

Name: gamma_T_aPDL1
Definition: Volume fraction of tumor interstitial space accessible to anti-PD-L1 antibody, determines intratumoral drug penetration for cancer cell checkpoint inhibition

Name: gamma_LN_aPDL1
Definition: Volume fraction of lymph node interstitial space accessible to anti-PD-L1 antibody, affects drug availability for APC checkpoint inhibition

Name: k_cln_aPDL1
Definition: Maximum non-linear clearance rate of anti-PD-L1 antibody from central compartment, represents target-mediated drug disposition at high PD-L1 expression

Name: Kc_aPDL1
Definition: Half-maximal anti-PD-L1 concentration for saturable clearance, determines transition from linear to non-linear pharmacokinetics

Name: q_P_aCTLA4
Definition: Capillary filtration rate of anti-CTLA-4 antibody from central to peripheral compartment, determines systemic drug distribution for checkpoint blockade

Name: q_T_aCTLA4
Definition: Capillary filtration rate of anti-CTLA-4 antibody from central to tumor compartment, determines tumor penetration for T cell costimulatory blockade

Name: q_LN_aCTLA4
Definition: Capillary filtration rate of anti-CTLA-4 antibody from central to tumor-draining lymph node compartment, enables T cell priming modulation

Name: q_LD_aCTLA4
Definition: Rate of anti-CTLA-4 antibody lymphatic drainage from tumor-draining lymph nodes back to central circulation, completes drug distribution cycle

Name: k_cl_aCTLA4
Definition: Systemic clearance rate of anti-CTLA-4 antibody from central compartment, determines drug half-life and dosing requirements for sustained costimulatory blockade

Name: gamma_C_aCTLA4
Definition: Volume fraction of central compartment interstitial space accessible to anti-CTLA-4 antibody, determines drug distribution volume and concentration

Name: gamma_P_aCTLA4
Definition: Volume fraction of peripheral compartment interstitial space accessible to anti-CTLA-4 antibody, affects tissue drug penetration and efficacy

Name: gamma_T_aCTLA4
Definition: Volume fraction of tumor interstitial space accessible to anti-CTLA-4 antibody, determines intratumoral drug penetration for T cell costimulatory blockade

Name: gamma_LN_aCTLA4
Definition: Volume fraction of lymph node interstitial space accessible to anti-CTLA-4 antibody, affects drug availability for T cell priming modulation

Name: kon_PD1_PDL1
Definition: Association rate constant for PD-1/PD-L1 binding in immunological synapse, determines checkpoint inhibition strength and T cell suppression kinetics

Name: k_out_PDL1
Definition: Rate of PD-L1 protein expression and translocation to cancer cell surface, enhanced by IFN-γ signaling (r_PDL1_IFNg fold increase)

Name: k_in_PDL1
Definition: Rate of PD-L1 internalization and degradation from cancer cell surface, determines steady-state PD-L1 density for checkpoint inhibition

Name: r_PDL1_IFNg
Definition: Fold increase in PD-L1 expression induced by IFN-γ secreted by activated CD8+ T cells, represents adaptive immune resistance mechanism

Name: kon_PD1_PDL2
Definition: Association rate constant for PD-1/PD-L2 binding in immunological synapse, alternative checkpoint pathway with similar inhibitory effects

Name: kon_PD1_aPD1
Definition: Association rate constant for PD-1 binding to anti-PD-1 antibody, determines drug binding kinetics and checkpoint blockade efficacy

Name: kon_PDL1_aPDL1
Definition: Association rate constant for PD-L1 binding to anti-PD-L1 antibody, determines drug binding kinetics and checkpoint blockade efficacy

Name: kon_CD28_CD80
Definition: Association rate constant for CD28-CD80 costimulatory binding in immunological synapse, determines T cell activation enhancement strength

Name: kon_CD28_CD86
Definition: Association rate constant for CD28-CD86 costimulatory binding in immunological synapse, alternative costimulatory pathway for T cell activation

Name: kon_CTLA4_CD80
Definition: Association rate constant for CTLA-4-CD80 inhibitory binding, competes with CD28 for CD80 and suppresses T cell activation

Name: kon_CTLA4_CD86
Definition: Association rate constant for CTLA-4-CD86 inhibitory binding, competes with CD28 for CD86 and suppresses T cell activation

Name: kon_CD80_PDL1
Definition: Association rate constant for CD80-PD-L1 cis-interaction on same cell surface, modulates costimulatory and checkpoint signaling balance

Name: kon_CTLA4_aCTLA4
Definition: Association rate constant for CTLA-4 binding to anti-CTLA-4 antibody, determines drug binding kinetics and costimulatory blockade efficacy

Name: kon_CD80_CD80
Definition: Association rate constant for CD80 homodimerization, required for high-affinity binding to CD28 and CTLA-4 receptors

Name: koff_PD1_PDL1
Definition: Dissociation rate constant for PD-1/PD-L1 complex, determines duration of checkpoint inhibition signal and T cell suppression strength

Name: koff_PD1_PDL2
Definition: Dissociation rate constant for PD-1/PD-L2 complex, alternative checkpoint pathway with longer binding duration than PD-L1

Name: koff_PD1_aPD1
Definition: Dissociation rate constant for PD-1/anti-PD-1 complex, very slow off-rate enables sustained checkpoint blockade with intermittent dosing

Name: koff_PDL1_aPDL1
Definition: Dissociation rate constant for PD-L1/anti-PD-L1 complex, extremely slow off-rate enables sustained checkpoint blockade with intermittent dosing

Name: koff_CD28_CD80
Definition: Dissociation rate constant for CD28-CD80 costimulatory complex, determines duration of T cell activation enhancement signals

Name: koff_CD28_CD86
Definition: Dissociation rate constant for CD28-CD86 costimulatory complex, faster off-rate than CD80 provides transient activation signals

Name: koff_CTLA4_CD80
Definition: Dissociation rate constant for CTLA-4-CD80 inhibitory complex, slower than CD28-CD80 enables competitive inhibition of costimulation

Name: koff_CTLA4_CD86
Definition: Dissociation rate constant for CTLA-4-CD86 inhibitory complex, faster than CD80 but still enables competitive inhibition of CD28 costimulation

Name: koff_CD80_PDL1
Definition: Dissociation rate constant for CD80-PD-L1 cis-interaction, rapid off-rate allows dynamic regulation of costimulatory/checkpoint balance

Name: koff_CTLA4_aCTLA4
Definition: Dissociation rate constant for CTLA-4/anti-CTLA-4 complex, slow off-rate enables sustained costimulatory blockade with intermittent dosing

Name: koff_CD80_CD80
Definition: Dissociation rate constant for CD80 homodimer, determines stability of active CD80 conformation for receptor binding

Name: Chi_PD1_aPD1
Definition: Bivalent anti-PD-1 antibody cross-arm binding efficiency parameter, accounts for avidity effects and 3D-to-2D binding conversion in immunological synapse

Name: Chi_PDL1_aPDL1
Definition: Bivalent anti-PD-L1 antibody cross-arm binding efficiency parameter, accounts for avidity effects and 3D-to-2D binding conversion in immunological synapse

Name: Chi_CTLA4_aCTLA4
Definition: Bivalent anti-CTLA-4 antibody cross-arm binding efficiency parameter, accounts for avidity effects and 3D-to-2D binding conversion in immunological synapse

Name: PD1_50
Definition: Half-maximal PD-1/PD-L1 complex density in immunological synapse for T cell inactivation, determines sensitivity of checkpoint inhibition to receptor expression

Name: n_PD1
Definition: Hill coefficient for PD-1/PD-L1 checkpoint inhibition dose-response curve, determines steepness of T cell suppression with receptor density

Name: CD28_CD8X_50
Definition: Half-maximal CD28-CD80/CD86 complex density in immunological synapse for T cell costimulation, accounts for CTLA-4 baseline inhibition effects

Name: n_CD28_CD8X
Definition: Hill coefficient for CD28-CD80/CD86 costimulation dose-response curve, determines steepness of T cell activation enhancement with receptor density

Name: T_PD1_total
Definition: Total number of PD-1 checkpoint receptors per T cell surface, determines maximum binding capacity for PD-L1/PD-L2 and anti-PD-1 antibodies
References: Cheng 2014, PMID: 23417675; Mkrtichyan 2012, PMID: 22837483; Gatalica 2014, PMID: 25392179

Name: T_CD28_total
Definition: Total number of CD28 costimulatory receptors per T cell surface, determines maximum binding capacity for CD80/CD86 and costimulation potential

Name: T_CTLA4_syn
Definition: Number of CTLA-4 inhibitory receptors per T cell in immunological synapse, competes with CD28 for CD80/CD86 binding and suppresses activation

Name: T_PDL1_total
Definition: Total number of PD-L1 molecules per activated T cell surface, enables reverse checkpoint signaling and T cell fratricide
References: Cheng 2014, PMID: 23417675; Mkrtichyan 2012, PMID: 22837483; Gatalica 2014, PMID: 25392179

Name: C1_PDL1_base
Definition: Baseline number of PD-L1 checkpoint ligands per chemotherapy-sensitive cancer cell surface, enhanced by IFN-γ (r_PDL1_IFNg fold) for adaptive immune resistance
References: Cheng 2014, PMID: 23417675; Mkrtichyan 2012, PMID: 22837483; Shin 2017, PMID: 27903500; Gatalica 2014, PMID: 25392179

Name: r_PDL2_C1
Definition: Ratio of PD-L2 to PD-L1 checkpoint ligands on chemotherapy-sensitive cancer cells, determines relative contribution of PD-L2 pathway to T cell inhibition

Name: C1_CD80_total
Definition: Total number of CD80 costimulatory ligands per chemotherapy-sensitive cancer cell surface, enables direct T cell costimulation or CTLA-4 inhibition

Name: C1_CD86_total
Definition: Total number of CD86 costimulatory ligands per chemotherapy-sensitive cancer cell surface, primary costimulatory ligand with higher expression than CD80

Name: syn_T_C1_PDL1_total
Definition: Total PD-L1 density in T cell-chemotherapy-sensitive cancer cell immunological synapse, calculated variable representing checkpoint ligand availability for PD-1 binding

Name: syn_T_C1_PDL2_total
Definition: Total PD-L2 density in T cell-chemotherapy-sensitive cancer cell immunological synapse, calculated variable representing alternative checkpoint ligand for PD-1 binding

Name: APC_PDL1_base
Definition: Baseline number of PD-L1 checkpoint ligands per antigen-presenting cell surface, provides inhibitory signals during T cell priming in lymph nodes
References: Cheng 2014, PMID: 23417675; Mkrtichyan 2012, PMID: 22837483; Shin 2017, PMID: 27903500; Gatalica 2014, PMID: 25392179

Name: r_PDL2APC
Definition: Ratio of PD-L2 to PD-L1 checkpoint ligands on antigen-presenting cells, determines relative contribution of PD-L2 pathway to T cell priming inhibition

Name: APC_CD80_total
Definition: Total number of CD80 costimulatory ligands per antigen-presenting cell surface, provides costimulatory signals for T cell activation and priming

Name: APC_CD86_total
Definition: Total number of CD86 costimulatory ligands per antigen-presenting cell surface, primary costimulatory ligand for T cell activation with higher expression than CD80

Name: syn_T_APC_PDL1_total
Definition: Total PD-L1 density in T cell-APC immunological synapse, calculated variable representing checkpoint ligand availability for inhibiting T cell priming

Name: syn_T_APC_PDL2_total
Definition: Total PD-L2 density in T cell-APC immunological synapse, calculated variable representing alternative checkpoint ligand for inhibiting T cell priming

Name: k_Th_act
Definition: Rate of T helper cell activation upon antigen presentation by APCs in lymph nodes, determines speed of adaptive immune response initiation

Name: k_Th_Treg
Definition: Rate of T helper cell differentiation to regulatory T cells, enhanced by TGF-β signaling and promotes immune suppression

Name: k_TGFb_Tsec
Definition: TGF secretion rate by Treg

Name: k_TGFb_deg
Definition: TGF-β degradation rate in tumor microenvironment, determines clearance of immunosuppressive cytokine

Name: TGFb_50
Definition: Half-maximal TGF-β concentration for Th-to-Treg differentiation, chemoresistance development, and M1-to-M2 macrophage polarization

Name: TGFb_50_Teff
Definition: Half-maximal TGF-β concentration for CD8+ T effector cell inhibition and functional suppression in tumor microenvironment
References: Estimated from T cell suppression assays

Name: Kc_rec
Definition: Half-Maximal cancer cell number for T cell recruitment

Name: TGFbase
Definition: Baseline TGF-β concentration in pancreatic tumor microenvironment, maintained by cancer cells and stromal components
References: Ivanović 2003, PMID: 12751375; Manuyakorn 2008, PMID: 19317336; Panis 2013, PMID: 23393376

Name: k_IFNg_Thsec
Definition: IFNg secretion rate by T helper cell estimated assuming IFNg secretion is 3 times lower in CD4 than CD8 T cells

Name: k_IFNg_deg
Definition: IFNg degradation rate

Name: IFNg_50_ind
Definition: Half-Maximal IFNg level for PD-L1 induction

Name: H_TGFb
Definition: Hill function for TGF-β-mediated effects on Th-to-Treg differentiation, chemoresistance development, and M1-to-M2 polarization
References: Calculated from TGF-β concentration and TGFb_50

Name: H_TGFb_Teff
Definition: Hill function for TGF-β-mediated CD8+ T effector cell inhibition and chemoresistance development
References: Calculated from TGF-β concentration and TGFb_50_Teff

Name: N_aTh
Definition: Number of activated T helper cell generations during clonal expansion, modulated by costimulation and IL-2 availability
References: Calculated from costimulatory signals and IL-2 levels

Name: k_CCL2_sec
Definition: Rate of CCL2 (MCP-1) secretion by cancer cells and MDSCs, promotes MDSC recruitment to tumor microenvironment

Name: k_CCL2_deg
Definition: Rate of CCL2 degradation in tumor microenvironment, determines chemoattractant clearance

Name: CCL2_50
Definition: Half-maximal CCL2 concentration for MDSC recruitment into tumor, determines sensitivity to chemoattractant gradients

Name: k_MDSC_rec
Definition: Rate of MDSC recruitment into tumor compartment, driven by CCL2 chemokine gradients and tumor-derived factors

Name: k_MDSC_death
Definition: Rate of MDSC death in tumor microenvironment, determines steady-state immunosuppressive cell population

Name: k_NO_deg
Definition: Rate of nitric oxide (NO) degradation in tumor microenvironment, determines clearance of immunosuppressive mediator

Name: k_ArgI_deg
Definition: Rate of arginase I (ArgI) degradation in tumor microenvironment, determines clearance of amino acid-depleting enzyme

Name: k_NO_sec
Definition: Rate of nitric oxide (NO) secretion by MDSCs, creates immunosuppressive microenvironment and inhibits T cell function

Name: k_ArgI_sec
Definition: Rate of arginase I secretion by MDSCs, depletes L-arginine and suppresses T cell proliferation and function

Name: ArgI_50_Teff
Definition: Half-maximal arginase I concentration for T effector cell inhibition and death through L-arginine depletion

Name: NO_50_Teff
Definition: Half-maximal nitric oxide concentration for T effector cell inhibition and apoptosis induction

Name: ArgI_50_Treg
Definition: Half-maximal arginase I concentration for Treg expansion and Th-to-Treg transdifferentiation

Name: H_NO
Definition: Hill function for nitric oxide-mediated inhibition of T effector cells
References: Calculated from NO concentration and NO_50_Teff

Name: H_ArgI_Teff
Definition: Hill function for arginase I-mediated inhibition of T effector cells through L-arginine depletion
References: Calculated from ArgI concentration and ArgI_50_Teff

Name: H_ArgI_Treg
Definition: Hill function for arginase I-mediated T helper to regulatory T cell transdifferentiation
References: Calculated from ArgI concentration and ArgI_50_Treg

Name: H_MDSC
Definition: Hill function for combined MDSC-mediated immunosuppressive effects on T effector cells

Name: k_a1_cabozantinib
Definition: First-order absorption rate constant for cabozantinib from fast absorption site to central compartment

Name: k_a2_cabozantinib
Definition: First-order absorption rate constant for cabozantinib from slow absorption site to central compartment

Name: k_cln_cabozantinib
Definition: Maximum rate of nonlinear clearance for cabozantinib from central compartment via saturable metabolism

Name: Kc_cabozantinib
Definition: Half-maximal concentration for nonlinear clearance of cabozantinib, represents Michaelis-Menten constant for saturable metabolism

Name: lagP1_cabozantinib
Definition: Lag time for fast absorption phase of cabozantinib into central compartment, represents dissolution and transit delays

Name: lagP2_cabozantinib
Definition: Lag time for slow absorption phase of cabozantinib into central compartment, represents extended-release formulation kinetics

Name: F_cabozantinib
Definition: Oral bioavailability fraction of cabozantinib reaching systemic circulation after first-pass metabolism

Name: q_P_cabozantinib
Definition: Inter-compartmental clearance rate for cabozantinib between central and peripheral compartments

Name: q_T_cabozantinib
Definition: Inter-compartmental clearance rate for cabozantinib between central and tumor compartments

Name: q_LN_cabozantinib
Definition: Inter-compartmental clearance rate for cabozantinib between central and lymph node compartments

Name: q_LD_cabozantinib
Definition: Lymphatic drainage clearance rate for cabozantinib from lymph node to central compartment

Name: gamma_C_cabozantinib
Definition: Volume fraction of cabozantinib available for distribution in central compartment

Name: gamma_P_cabozantinib
Definition: Volume fraction of cabozantinib available for distribution in peripheral compartment

Name: gamma_T_cabozantinib
Definition: Volume fraction of cabozantinib available for distribution in tumor compartment

Name: gamma_LN_cabozantinib
Definition: Volume fraction of cabozantinib available for distribution in lymph node compartment

Name: IC50_MET
Definition: Half-maximal inhibitory concentration of cabozantinib for MET receptor tyrosine kinase, involved in cancer cell growth and invasion
References: Fitted from in vitro binding assays

Name: IC50_RET
Definition: Half-maximal inhibitory concentration of cabozantinib for RET receptor tyrosine kinase, involved in cancer cell survival signaling
References: Fitted from in vitro binding assays

Name: IC50_AXL
Definition: Half-maximal inhibitory concentration of cabozantinib for AXL receptor tyrosine kinase, promotes cancer cell survival and drug resistance
References: Fitted from in vitro binding assays

Name: IC50_VEGFR2
Definition: Half-maximal inhibitory concentration of cabozantinib for VEGFR2, inhibits tumor angiogenesis and vascular development
References: Fitted from in vitro binding assays

Name: k_C_resist
Definition: Rate of cancer cell transition to cabozantinib-resistant phenotype, resembles cancer stem cell characteristics

Name: k_K_cabo
Definition: Cabozantinib-mediated inhibition factor for maximal tumor carrying capacity through anti-angiogenic effects
References: Estimated from tumor growth inhibition data

Name: R_cabo
Definition: Resistance factor for cabozantinib efficacy based on AXL receptor expression levels
References: Calculated from cabozantinib concentration and IC50_AXL

Name: H_therapy_cabo
Definition: Hill function for cabozantinib therapeutic effects on tumor vasculature and angiogenesis
References: Calculated from cabozantinib concentration and vascular targets

Name: k_Mac_rec
Definition: Rate of macrophage recruitment into tumor compartment, driven by chemokine gradients and tumor-derived factors

Name: k_Mac_death
Definition: Rate of macrophage death in tumor microenvironment, determines steady-state macrophage population

Name: k_TGFb_Msec
Definition: Rate of TGF-β secretion by M2 macrophages in tumor compartment, promotes immunosuppression and tissue remodeling

Name: k_vas_Msec
Definition: Rate of angiogenic factor secretion by M2 macrophages, promotes tumor vascularization and growth

Name: k_IL12_sec
Definition: Rate of IL-12 secretion by mature antigen-presenting cells in tumor, promotes Th1 response and M1 macrophage polarization

Name: k_IL12_Msec
Definition: Rate of IL-12 secretion by M1 macrophages in tumor compartment, promotes anti-tumor immune responses

Name: k_IL12_deg
Definition: Rate of IL-12 degradation in tumor microenvironment, determines clearance of pro-inflammatory cytokine

Name: k_IL10_sec
Definition: Rate of IL-10 secretion by M2 macrophages in tumor compartment, promotes immunosuppression and M1-to-M2 polarization

Name: k_IL10_deg
Definition: Rate of IL-10 degradation in tumor microenvironment, determines clearance of anti-inflammatory cytokine

Name: k_M2_pol
Definition: Rate of M1 to M2 macrophage polarization driven by IL-10 and TGF-β, promotes tumor-supportive phenotype

Name: k_M1_pol
Definition: Rate of M2 to M1 macrophage polarization driven by IL-12 and IFN-γ, promotes anti-tumor phenotype

Name: IL10_50
Definition: Half-maximal IL-10 concentration for M1-to-M2 macrophage polarization, Treg maintenance, and mAPC inhibition via STAT6 signaling

Name: IL12_50
Definition: Half-maximal IL-12 concentration for M2-to-M1 macrophage polarization and pro-inflammatory activation

Name: IFNg_50
Definition: Half-maximal IFN-γ concentration for M2-to-M1 macrophage polarization and classical activation

Name: k_M1_phago
Definition: Rate of M1 macrophage-mediated phagocytosis and killing of cancer cells, represents anti-tumor effector function

Name: vol_Mcell
Definition: Volume of a single macrophage calculated from average diameter, used for concentration conversions

Name: kon_CD47_SIRPa
Definition: Association rate constant for CD47-SIRPα binding, initiates 'don't eat me' signal to inhibit phagocytosis

Name: koff_CD47_SIRPa
Definition: Dissociation rate constant for CD47-SIRPα binding complex

Name: SIRPa_50
Definition: Half-maximal SIRPα occupancy for CD47/SIRPα-mediated inhibition of cancer cell phagocytosis by macrophages

Name: n_SIRPa
Definition: Hill coefficient for CD47/SIRPα-mediated inhibition of cancer cell phagocytosis, determines cooperativity of inhibitory signaling

Name: H_Mac_C
Definition: Hill function for combined checkpoint inhibition effects on macrophage-mediated cancer cell phagocytosis

Name: H_PD1_M
Definition: Hill function for PD-1-mediated inhibition of macrophage phagocytosis through checkpoint signaling

Name: H_SIRPa
Definition: Hill function for SIRPα-mediated inhibition of macrophage phagocytosis through CD47 'don't eat me' signaling

Name: C_CD47
Definition: CD47 surface density on cancer cells, provides 'don't eat me' signal to inhibit macrophage phagocytosis

Name: M_PD1_total
Definition: Total PD-1 receptors per macrophage surface, mediates checkpoint inhibition of phagocytic function
References: Cheng 2014, PMID: 23417675; Mkrtichyan 2012, PMID: 22837483; Gordon 2017, PMID: 28514441

Name: M_SIRPa
Definition: SIRPα receptor density on macrophage surface, receives CD47 'don't eat me' signals from cancer cells

Name: A_Mcell
Definition: Surface area of a single macrophage calculated from average diameter, used for receptor density calculations

Name: IL10_50_phago
Definition: Half-maximal IL-10 concentration for inhibition of macrophage phagocytic activity

Name: K_Mac_C
Definition: Association coefficient for macrophage-cancer cell binding in immunological synapse formation

Name: M_total
Definition: Total macrophage population in tumor compartment (M1 + M2)

Name: H_IL10
Definition: Hill function for IL-10-mediated M2 macrophage polarization, Treg functionality, and antigen presentation inhibition
References: Calculated from IL-10 concentration and IL10_50

Name: H_IL10_phago
Definition: Hill function for IL-10-mediated inhibition of macrophage phagocytic activity
References: Calculated from IL-10 concentration and IL10_50_phago

Name: H_IL12
Definition: Hill function for IL-12-mediated M2-to-M1 macrophage polarization
References: Calculated from IL-12 concentration and IL12_50

Name: k_fib_rec
Definition: Rate of fibroblast recruitment into tumor microenvironment

Name: k_fib_const
Definition: Steady-state fibroblast concentration in tumor microenvironment

Name: k_caf_tran
Definition: Rate of normal fibroblast transformation to cancer-associated fibroblasts (CAFs)
References: Fitted to Ma 2022 single cell data

Name: k_ECM_fib_sec
Definition: Rate of extracellular matrix secretion by normal fibroblasts

Name: k_ECM_CAF_sec
Definition: Rate of extracellular matrix secretion by cancer-associated fibroblasts, higher than normal fibroblasts

Name: k_ECM_deg
Definition: Rate of extracellular matrix degradation in tumor microenvironment

Name: ECM_base
Definition: Baseline extracellular matrix concentration in normal pancreatic tissue

Name: ECM_max
Definition: Maximum extracellular matrix concentration in fibrotic pancreatic tumors, represents severe desmoplastic reaction

Name: ECM_level
Definition: Current ECM concentration in tumor, dynamically updated based on fibroblast/CAF secretion and degradation

Name: ECM_MW
Definition: Molecular weight of collagen (major ECM component), used for mass-to-volume conversion in tumor calculations

Name: ECM_density
Definition: Physical density of extracellular matrix components, used in tumor volume calculations for desmoplastic contribution

Name: k_fib_death
Definition: Rate of normal fibroblast death in tumor microenvironment, determines steady-state fibroblast population

Name: k_CAF_death
Definition: Rate of cancer-associated fibroblast death in tumor compartment, determines steady-state CAF population

Name: ECM_50_T_exh
Definition: Half-maximal ECM concentration for T cell exhaustion induction, determines sensitivity of CD8+ T cells to ECM-mediated functional impairment
References: Fitted to Ma 2022 single cell data

Name: ECM_50_T_mot
Definition: Half-maximal ECM concentration for reduced T cell motility and killing efficiency, represents physical barrier effects of dense collagen

Name: vol_Fibcell
Definition: Volume of a single normal fibroblast cell, used in tumor volume calculations

Name: vol_CAFcell
Definition: Volume of a single cancer-associated fibroblast cell, used in tumor volume calculations
